title: General Crisis Intervention Hotlines * Disaster/Emergency Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > General Crisis Intervention Hotlines * Disaster/Emergency Issues 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues At Risk Families Disaster/Emergency Issues Older Adults Transgender Individuals Youth Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF TRAUMA INTERVENTION PROGRAM NW Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Trained and screened volunteers provide emotional and practical support to people involved in traumatic events and their families in the first few hours following a tragedy: complete follow-up calls, information, and referral to other resources (burial services, probate information, hazardous clean up, and other services). Volunteers are requested to crisis scenes by police officers, firefighters, hospital personnel, organizations/businesses, and schools after a traumatic event. Eligibility: People involved in traumatic (i.e., an unexpected death) or crisis events and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 940-7997 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
